description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 reliability cluster: incident procedure authoring vs paging design vs alert wiring vs rollback authoring, and against the shipped systematic-debugger and regression-suite-curator.

cases.prompt: Define our severity levels, who runs incidents, the status-page templates, and the postmortem process.
cases.reason: Severity ladder, roles, comms, and postmortem machinery are the incident runbook's exact scope — the human procedures behind the pages others design and wire.

cases.prompt: Decide which conditions deserve to wake someone at night versus land as tickets.
cases.reason: The paging policy (symptom/budget-burn design) is reliability design; the runbook answers the page after that policy fires it.

cases.prompt: Write the exact backout steps for reverting the release — artifact IDs, command sequence, verification.
cases.reason: The rollback procedure itself is rollback-runbook-author's artifact; the incident runbook only states WHEN to invoke it and with what authority.

cases.prompt: The alert fired and the dashboard shows errors climbing — walk me through finding the cause right now.
cases.reason: Live root-cause work is the debugger's reproduce→isolate method; the runbook's triage hands off to it at the decision point.

cases.prompt: Last week's incident produced three bug fixes — get them into the regression suite at the right tier.
cases.reason: Executing the promotion of incident findings into suite tiers is the curator's casework; the runbook's postmortem only mandates that handoff.
